# What is GitStack? A Founder's Guide to Open-Source Tool Discovery

Building a startup used to mean spending thousands of dollars on SaaS subscriptions. GitStack changes the game by helping non-technical founders discover and deploy free, open-source alternatives to expensive software.

## The Problem: SaaS Costs Are Crushing Founders

The average startup spends over $20,000/year on software before making a single dollar. Email tools, CRMs, analytics, scheduling — it all adds up fast.

## The Solution: Open Source, Simplified

GitStack is the layer between GitHub tools existing and you actually using them. We:

- **Curate** 600+ open-source tools reviewed for founder-friendliness
- **Explain** complex repos in plain English using AI
- **Generate** complete tech stacks tailored to your idea
- **Translate** any GitHub repo into a non-technical brief

## Key Features

### Stack Generator
Tell us what you're building, and we'll recommend the perfect open-source stack.

### Repo Translator
Paste any GitHub URL and get a plain-English explanation of what it does.

### Dead Tool Detector
Avoid abandoned projects. We flag repos that haven't been updated recently.

## Who Is GitStack For?

- Non-technical founders who want to build without developers
- Indie hackers looking to reduce monthly burn
- Small teams who need enterprise-grade tools on a $0 budget

## Get Started

Visit [gitstack.pro](https://gitstack.pro) and try the Stack Generator — no signup required.

posts.title: top-5-zapier-alternatives
posts.author: GitStack
posts.body:
---
title: "Top 5 Free Open-Source Alternatives to Zapier"
description: "Replace Zapier with these powerful free automation tools. Self-host your workflows and save thousands per year."
date: "2026-05-09"
author: "GitStack Team"
tags: ["automation", "zapier", "n8n", "open-source", "alternatives"]
image: "/og-image.svg"
---

# Top 5 Free Open-Source Alternatives to Zapier

Zapier is great — until you hit their pricing. At $20–$100/month for serious automation, costs add up fast. Here are 5 free, open-source alternatives that give you the same power without the monthly bill.

## 1. n8n — The Visual Workflow Builder

n8n is the closest thing to Zapier in the open-source world. It features a drag-and-drop interface, 400+ integrations, and runs on your own server.

**Best for:** Non-technical founders who want Zapier-like ease with full control.

**Setup time:** 15 minutes on Railway or Render.

## 2. Huginn — The "Personal Agent" Automator

Huginn lets you build agents that monitor events and act on them. Think of it as a programmable IFTTT that runs locally.

**Best for:** Technical users who want custom logic and data scraping.

## 3. Activepieces — The Modern Alternative

Activepieces is a newer entrant with a clean UI and strong community. It supports loops, branching, and custom code pieces.

**Best for:** Teams that need collaboration features and modern UX.

## 4. Node-RED — IoT + Automation Powerhouse

Built by IBM, Node-RED is incredibly powerful for connecting APIs, hardware, and services. The flow-based programming is intuitive once you get the hang of it.

**Best for:** Founders building hardware-software products or complex API chains.

## 5. AutoHotkey / AutoIt (Windows) or Hammerspoon (Mac)

For desktop automation, these scripting tools can replicate many Zapier use cases locally — file handling, app launching, text expansion.

**Best for:** Solo founders who need quick desktop automation without cloud complexity.

## Cost Comparison

| Tool | Monthly Cost (SaaS) | Self-Hosted Cost |
|------|---------------------|------------------|
| Zapier | $20–$100 | N/A |
| n8n | $0 | $5/server |
| Huginn | $0 | $5/server |
| Activepieces | $0 | $5/server |

## Bottom Line

If you're spending $50+/month on Zapier, switching to n8n or Activepieces could save you $600/year while giving you more control. Use GitStack's Stack Generator to get a complete self-hosted automation stack.

posts.title: how-to-self-host-n8n
posts.author: GitStack
posts.body:
---
title: "How to Self-Host n8n in 10 Minutes"
description: "Step-by-step guide to deploying n8n on Railway, Render, or Docker. Start automating for free today."
date: "2026-05-08"
author: "GitStack Team"
tags: ["n8n", "self-hosting", "automation", "tutorial", "railway"]
image: "/og-image.svg"
---

# How to Self-Host n8n in 10 Minutes

n8n is the most popular open-source alternative to Zapier. This guide walks you through deploying it on Railway — the fastest, easiest method.

## Prerequisites

- A Railway account (free tier works)
- A GitHub account

## Step 1: Fork the n8n Template

Railway has a one-click n8n template. Search "n8n" in Railway's template library and click Deploy.

## Step 2: Configure Environment Variables

Set these required variables:

- `N8N_BASIC_AUTH_ACTIVE`: true
- `N8N_BASIC_AUTH_USER`: your-username
- `N8N_BASIC_AUTH_PASSWORD`: your-secure-password
- `WEBHOOK_URL`: Your Railway domain (e.g., https://n8n-production.up.railway.app)

## Step 3: Deploy

Click Deploy. Railway will build and start n8n automatically. In 2–3 minutes, visit your domain and log in.

## Step 4: Create Your First Workflow

1. Click "Add Workflow"
2. Choose a trigger (e.g., Schedule, Webhook, or HTTP Request)
3. Add nodes for the services you want to connect
4. Activate the workflow

## Alternative: Docker Deploy

If you prefer full control, run:

```bash
docker run -it --rm \
  --name n8n \
  -p 5678:5678 \
  -v ~/.n8n:/home/node/.n8n \
  n8nio/n8n
```

## Next Steps

- Set up HTTPS with a custom domain
- Configure SMTP for email notifications
- Explore the 400+ built-in integrations

Need help choosing the right automation stack? Use GitStack's Stack Generator to get a personalized recommendation.

posts.title: build-saas-for-free
posts.author: GitStack
posts.body:
---
title: "Build Your First SaaS for $0 Using Open Source"
description: "The complete blueprint for launching a software business using only free, open-source tools. No coding budget required."
date: "2026-05-07"
author: "GitStack Team"
tags: ["saas", "founders", "no-code", "open-source", "startup"]
image: "/og-image.svg"
---

# Build Your First SaaS for $0 Using Open Source

Every SaaS founder's nightmare: spending $500/month on tools before making a single dollar. Here's how to build a complete tech stack for exactly $0.

## The $0 Stack Blueprint

### Authentication: Keycloak or Supabase Auth
Instead of Auth0 ($25/month), use Keycloak or Supabase's built-in auth. Both support social logins, MFA, and user management.

### Database: PostgreSQL + Supabase
Self-hosted PostgreSQL is free forever. Supabase adds a generous free tier with real-time subscriptions.

### Backend: Directus or Strapi
Need a CMS + API? Directus and Strapi give you a full admin panel and REST/GraphQL APIs without writing backend code.

### Frontend: Next.js or Nuxt
React and Vue frameworks are free and deploy easily to Vercel or Netlify's free tiers.

### Payments: Stripe (pay-as-you-earn)
Stripe takes a percentage only when you make money. No monthly fees.

### Email: Listmonk or Mailtrain
Replace Mailchimp ($20/month) with Listmonk. Self-hosted, beautiful, and handles newsletters + transactional emails.

### Analytics: Plausible or Umami
Google Analytics is free but privacy-invasive. Plausible and Umami are lightweight, GDPR-compliant, and self-hostable.

### CRM: Twenty or ERPNext
Twenty is a modern open-source CRM. ERPNext handles CRM, inventory, accounting, and more.

## Deployment Strategy

Use Railway, Render, or Fly.io free tiers for hosting. As you grow, scale gradually.

## Real Cost Comparison

| Component | SaaS Cost | Open Source Cost |
|-----------|-----------|------------------|
| Auth | $25/mo | $0 |
| Database | $15/mo | $0 |
| CMS/API | $25/mo | $0 |
| Email | $20/mo | $0 |
| CRM | $30/mo | $0 |
| **Total** | **$115/mo** | **$0** |

## The Catch

Open source requires setup time. But with GitStack's Stack Generator and setup guides, you can deploy a complete stack in under 2 hours.

## Start Building

Use GitStack to generate your personalized $0 tech stack. Tell us what you're building — we'll handle the rest.
